pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py explicit checks
api.vitaldb.net/cases/6 returns 403 Forbidden, but /0006.vital returns a valid file matching the md5sum of the 0006.vital file found in the workspace.
The setup_task.ps1 script is entirely missing from the workspace, so the file is never actually placed at this path.
The setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1 scripts are missing from the workspace, so this file is never generated, rendering the verifier partially defective.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real VitalDB open dataset data for case 6. The actual download URL for the file is https://api.vitaldb.net/0006.vital, which matches the MD5 hash (526dbef6a38b42526fdd091d0e827bc2) of the 0006.vital file found in the workspace, contradicting the claimed /cases/6 URL which returns a 403 Forbidden error. By analyzing the .vital file directly via the python vitaldb package, the actual shortest tracks in case 6 are 'BIS/SR', 'BIS/SEF', and 'BIS/TOTPOW' (all 57.80 minutes), with a total case duration of 85.75 minutes. The expected report path is verified as C:\Users\Docker\Documents\shortest_track_report.txt. However, the task is heavily defective: setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1 are missing from the environment entirely, meaning the data file is never placed at the claimed path and the ground truth JSON is never generated for the verifier.
